Patch Set 19:

Hi Kapil, great refactoring, looks much better now. Whats the reason behind removing scaling of pictures and set fixed size (500px most of the time)? Some pictures appear quite small now, see e.g. https://docs.onap.org/projects/onap-ccsdk-cds/en/latest/userguide/running-bp-processor-in-ide.html

Patch Set 19:

> Patch Set 19:
> 
> Hi Kapil, great refactoring, looks much better now. Whats the reason behind removing scaling of pictures and set fixed size (500px most of the time)? Some pictures appear quite small now, see e.g. https://docs.onap.org/projects/onap-ccsdk-cds/en/latest/userguide/running-bp-processor-in-ide.html

Defined width in px or in was not making picture scalable, docs was destorted on smaller screen (phone, stacked/cascaded view on System)

Changing it to 'pt' makes picture scale according to screen size.

Regarding width 500pt, I kept is same so that all pics looks alligned on page, and if required indiviual pictures can be clicked to have it's actual size view 😊

Patch Set 19:

> Patch Set 19:
> 
> > Patch Set 19:
> > 
> > Hi Kapil, great refactoring, looks much better now. Whats the reason behind removing scaling of pictures and set fixed size (500px most of the time)? Some pictures appear quite small now, see e.g. https://docs.onap.org/projects/onap-ccsdk-cds/en/latest/userguide/running-bp-processor-in-ide.html
> 
> Defined width in px or in was not making picture scalable, docs was destorted on smaller screen (phone, stacked/cascaded view on System)
> 
> Changing it to 'pt' makes picture scale according to screen size.
> 
> Regarding width 500pt, I kept is same so that all pics looks alligned on page, and if required indiviual pictures can be clicked to have it's actual size view 😊

Oh i've read px instead of pt. Then its fine..
